author: Agnes Martin
title: The Untroubled Mind
excerpt: Art is the concrete representation of our most subtle feelings.

author: Ad Reinhardt
title: Art-as-Art
excerpt: Art is art-as-art and everything else is everything else.

author: Mark Rothko
title: The Romantics Were Prompted
excerpt: A painting is not a picture of an experience; it is an experience.

author: Susan Sontag
title: Against Interpretation
excerpt: In place of a hermeneutics we need an erotics of art.

author: Sol LeWitt
title: Sentences on Conceptual Art
excerpt: Banal ideas cannot be rescued by beautiful execution.

author: John Berger
title: Ways of Seeing
excerpt: Publicity speaks in the future tense.

author: Boris Groys
title: Art Power
excerpt: The artwork resists consumption by suspending its use-value.

author: Rosalind E. Krauss
title: Sculpture in the Expanded Field
excerpt: Purity is not a condition but an argument staged within form.
